Draft the buffer stock explainer for the next Substack post

evidence.note: Collected from the printer, boxed in the office.

notes.title: Buffer Stock Theory
notes.content:
# Buffer Stock Theory

The core move: stabilise a price by holding a **buffer stock** of the thing itself.

- Wool floor price scheme: buffer stock of wool
- [[Job Guarantee]]: buffer stock of *employed people*
- Unemployment (NAIRU): buffer stock of *unemployed people* — the cruel anchor

> The question is never *whether* we run a buffer stock. It is *which one*, and *who pays the carrying cost*.

See [[Monetary Sovereignty]] for why the issuer can always finance the superior buffer.

notes.title: Job Guarantee
notes.content:
# Job Guarantee

A standing public option for work at a fixed wage. Anchors the price level from below while delivering public value.

Links back to [[Buffer Stock Theory]] — the JG *is* the employed buffer stock.

Campaign asset: **Card 04** pairs this with basic income framing for the doorstep.

notes.title: Monetary Sovereignty
notes.content:
# Monetary Sovereignty

Australia is a **Source Anchor**: free-floating key currency, own-currency debt, full central-bank capacity.

Implication for the doorstep: *sovereign yields reflect policy stance, not credit risk*. The household-budget analogy is a category error — see [[Buffer Stock Theory]].
